milestones.title: Enriched Market Intelligence Experience
milestones.vision: Keep the French-first M002 product surfaces delivered in S01-S09, but replace the underlying monolithic SQLite boundary with a production-grade polyglot data platform that can retain raw evidence cheaply, serve large-corpus analytics at low latency, keep mutable runtime truth bounded, and prepare grounded AI without another future storage rearchitecture.
success_criteria: The default home on the live radar remains a French market overview that tells a broad audience what is moving now, what deserves attention, and how trustworthy the read currently is.
The home, explorer, and detail surfaces no longer depend on request-time full-corpus Python or SQLite history scans for their primary user paths.
Discovery continues to push both minimum and maximum price bounds directly into the Vinted catalog API while preserving local correctness guards.
A desktop user can explore the real tracked corpus with server-side filtering, sorting, and paging across category, brand, price band, condition, and sold state.

A listing detail explains why the listing matters in plain language before exposing technical proof, while preserving the boundary between observed facts, inferred state, estimated publication timing, and radar timestamps.
The runtime/admin surface truthfully shows running, paused, scheduled, failed, elapsed pause time, next resume timing, and recent errors from persisted runtime state.
The live VPS-served radar remains consultable from both phone and desktop through a responsive web experience.
When acquisition weakens or signals become partial, the product stays explicit about degraded coverage, freshness, confidence, and uncertainty instead of implying false precision.
The live collector no longer uses one monolithic SQLite database as the durable boundary for mutable runtime state, analytical serving, and raw evidence retention.
Mutable current-state and control-plane truth run on PostgreSQL, analytical facts and serving rollups run on ClickHouse, and immutable raw evidence lands in S3-compatible Parquet storage.
Retention, TTL, and reconciliation keep hot storage bounded while preserving auditable raw evidence and historical analytical continuity.
The new platform exposes AI-ready feature and evidence marts so future grounded AI work does not require another storage-layer redesign.

key_risks.risk: The current SQLite schema duplicates heavyweight card payload JSON across mutable and historical tables, so each successful cycle can still consume unacceptable disk even when runtime behavior is otherwise healthy.
key_risks.whyItMatters: This was the direct production failure mode on the VPS; leaving it unresolved makes every other product gain operationally fragile.

key_risks.risk: Moving to a polyglot data platform can easily create data drift between mutable state, analytical facts, and raw evidence if the event/outbox contract is weak.
key_risks.whyItMatters: A storage split only helps if the three layers stay reconcilable; otherwise the product becomes faster but less trustworthy.

key_risks.risk: Historical backfill from SQLite into PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and Parquet could silently lose continuity or skew derived metrics.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The product promise depends on traceable history; a bad backfill would invalidate comparisons, cadence, and future AI grounding.

key_risks.risk: The current application code concentrates persistence and querying inside SQLite-oriented seams, so a naive migration could create a bigger god-repository instead of a cleaner platform boundary.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If we do not introduce proper ports/adapters and projectors, the new architecture will be technically stronger on paper but harder to evolve than the current one.

key_risks.risk: Retention, TTL, and proof-shape policy could be under-specified, causing ClickHouse or object storage to become the next unbounded sink.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The whole point of the rearchitecture is to keep the system bounded without sacrificing evidence; lifecycle mistakes would repeat the same failure on a different backend.

key_risks.risk: Product read paths may accidentally keep hidden dependencies on SQLite-era query semantics during cutover.
key_risks.whyItMatters: That would leave the live app in a mixed state where some screens are fast and others still hinge on the old bottleneck.

key_risks.risk: The platform could become technically impressive but over-complicated for the actual runtime if we add unnecessary infrastructure beyond the minimum needed split.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The goal is the best durable system, not maximal component count; complexity without clear responsibility boundaries would hurt operability.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The current home/dashboard path still materializes listing state inputs across the full corpus and scores in Python on each request.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The default home runs on SQL-backed overview payloads with visible honesty/freshness signals instead of full-corpus request-time recomputation.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The persisted runtime model does not honestly represent paused, scheduled, failed, and next-resume truth.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Runtime state is first-class in persistence, API, CLI, health, and the runtime page.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The product shell and VPS entrypoint are not yet coherent or reliable for phone/desktop remote consultation.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Mounted/public serving works through one shared shell and route contract on phone and desktop.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Explorer-scale browsing and comparison logic will become too expensive as the corpus grows if the product keeps broad request-time reshaping.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A user can browse, filter, sort, and compare the real tracked corpus through SQL-first explorer contracts.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Listing detail can remain technically accurate but too debugger-shaped for daily product use.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Detail pages lead with plain-language narrative while preserving proof and provenance boundaries.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Weak acquisition or anti-bot friction could silently degrade the product read.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Degraded/partial acquisition truth is explicit across product, runtime, and health surfaces.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The assembled product could still fail at realistic live VPS usage even if slice-level local proofs pass.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Overview, explorer, detail, runtime, and degraded-mode honesty work end to end on the real VPS and realistic corpus.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Discovery still wastes request budget if price filters remain only client-side.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Native API-side `price_from` / `price_to` are used while local correctness guards remain truthful.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The collector still underuses a large proxy pool and leaves operators with weak transport visibility.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A large Webshare proxy pool can drive real multi-route throughput with safe metadata and preflight-backed operator checks.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The codebase lacks a clean platform boundary for PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, object storage, migrations, and reliable multi-sink delivery.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The repo can boot the final data-platform dependencies, validate configuration, and emit versioned idempotent event/outbox/manifests contracts.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Raw evidence is still too heavy and too duplicated to leave the hot path bounded.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Discovery/state-refresh persist minimal evidence fragments to partitioned Parquet manifests on object storage instead of duplicating heavyweight raw payload JSON in mutable tables.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Mutable runtime truth and current-state writes still depend on the same SQLite boundary that just failed operationally.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Runtime control, discovery runs, catalogs, and listing current-state projections operate correctly on PostgreSQL-backed writes and backfills.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Analytical serving still depends on SQLite history scans rather than a low-latency columnar serving layer.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Overview, explorer, detail, and AI-ready analytical marts read from ClickHouse raw facts and rollups with acceptable latency and truthful drill-down.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Historical continuity and live app behavior may drift during migration and cutover.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: SQLite history is backfilled/reconciled into the new platform, reads are cut over end to end, and the live loop no longer depends on heavyweight SQLite history tables.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Even after cutover, the new platform could drift or grow unbounded without explicit lifecycle and audit controls.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Retention, TTL, reconciliation, and AI-ready feature/evidence marts keep the platform bounded, auditable, and ready for grounded intelligence work.

milestones.verification_contract: Contract verification now covers both the already-delivered product contracts and the new platform seams: parser/payload normalization tests, event-envelope/outbox/manifests tests, PostgreSQL schema/projector tests, ClickHouse schema/query tests, object-store manifest tests, and route/query contract assertions for overview, explorer, detail, runtime, CLI, and health.
milestones.verification_integration: Integration verification now includes end-to-end collector writes into PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and object storage; historical backfill/reconciliation from SQLite; overview/explorer/detail/runtime routes exercised on the cut-over platform; and live proxy-backed collection plus product serving against the real integrated stack.
milestones.verification_operational: Operational verification now includes platform bootstrap/readiness, delivery lag visibility, lifecycle/retention jobs, reconciliation health, cutover state, VPS long-run behavior, and restart/recovery proof on the new platform as well as the original product accessibility checks.
milestones.verification_uat: UAT verification now requires that the product still feels coherent and truthful to a French-speaking user on phone and desktop after the platform cutover, while operators can understand collector/runtime state, platform health, and evidence provenance without reading internal storage details.
definition_of_done: Every slice below is complete with substantive implementation rather than placeholder docs or scaffolding-only claims.
The live collector no longer depends on a monolithic SQLite history boundary for mutable runtime truth, analytical serving, and raw evidence retention at the same time.

PostgreSQL holds mutable control-plane/current-state truth, ClickHouse serves analytical product queries, and S3-compatible Parquet storage retains immutable raw evidence through one coherent event/manifests contract.
Overview, explorer, detail, runtime, CLI, and health surfaces operate correctly after the read/write cutover to the new platform.
Retention, reconciliation, and observability keep hot stores bounded while preserving auditability and future AI readiness.
Historical SQLite data needed for continuity has been backfilled or explicitly retired under a documented and verified cutover policy.
The final integrated acceptance is re-proven against the real live platform rather than against the retired SQLite-heavy path.
milestones.requirement_coverage: M002 still directly covers the already-delivered product requirements R004, R007, R009, R010, R011, and R012. The new S10–S15 extension adds the missing durable data-platform foundation required to keep those capabilities credible under real growth, while also advancing the architectural prerequisites for R002 continuity, R013 product-level grouping, R014 grounded AI insights, R015 AI-assisted exploration, and R016 SaaS hardening without another future storage rewrite.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 → S03 foundational product path

Produces:
- SQL-backed overview home, truthful runtime snapshotting, and the mounted French-first shell that define the current M002 product contract
- stable route, payload, and vocabulary seams for overview, explorer, detail, runtime, and health

Consumes:
- M001 listing/state/scoring/runtime evidence backbone
- the existing Python service boundary and brownfield repository/WSGI seams

### S04 → S07 product completion path

Produces:
- the scalable explorer workspace, narrative detail route, degraded-acquisition honesty contract, and realistic live VPS proof for the current product surface
- the current public browsing/admin experience already proven on real data

Consumes:
- the S01–S03 overview/runtime/shell foundation
- live acquisition/runtime behavior from the SQLite-era collector

### S08 → S09 acquisition throughput path

Produces:
- native API price-window control and a high-throughput proxy-pool operator path that materially improved collection throughput
- the current acquisition/runtime posture that the new platform must preserve while changing storage boundaries

Consumes:
- degraded-mode/runtime honesty from S06
- the live operator path proven in S07

### S09 → S10 platform foundation

Produces:
- provider-agnostic platform configuration, connection/bootstrap contracts, schema migration runners, and an idempotent event/outbox/manifests seam for the new data plane
- one authoritative place where collector/runtime code resolves PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and object-storage dependencies

Consumes:
- the proven M002 product and acquisition contracts from S01–S09
- the post-S09 storage-growth incident that invalidated SQLite as the long-term durable boundary

### S10 → S11 raw evidence path

Produces:
- a minimal evidence contract and partitioned Parquet object-store lake for immutable raw listing/probe evidence
- manifest-backed proof retrieval that removes heavyweight raw payload duplication from hot mutable tables

Consumes:
- platform bootstrap, event envelope, and manifest seams from S10
- current parser/card payload normalization logic from the SQLite-era collector

### S10 + S11 → S12 mutable truth path

Produces:
- PostgreSQL-backed control-plane and current-state truth for runtime state, catalogs, discovery runs, listing current state, and recent presence projections
- projector/backfill seams that stop the live loop from mutating heavyweight SQLite history tables

Consumes:
- platform/outbox seams from S10
- raw evidence/lake manifests from S11

### S10 + S11 + S12 → S13 analytical serving path

Produces:
- ClickHouse raw fact tables, materialized rollups, and serving marts for overview/explorer/detail analytics
- a low-latency analytical boundary separate from mutable runtime truth

Consumes:
- event/outbox contracts from S10
- minimal raw evidence and manifests from S11
- PostgreSQL current-state/control-plane truth from S12

### S12 + S13 → S14 cutover path

Produces:
- historical backfill from SQLite into PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and the Parquet lake
- end-to-end application read cutover plus runtime/collector cutover off heavyweight SQLite history tables

Consumes:
- PostgreSQL mutable truth from S12
- ClickHouse serving marts from S13
- historical SQLite data as migration input only

### S14 → S15 lifecycle and AI-readiness path

Produces:
- retention/TTL/compaction policies, reconciliation/audit surfaces, and AI-ready feature/evidence marts that keep the new platform bounded and trustworthy
- the durable platform contract required before grounded AI and SaaS hardening work can proceed without another storage rearchitecture

Consumes:
- the cut-over live platform from S14
- the product/adoption goals that follow in M003 and M004

# S10: Polyglot Data Platform Foundation

**S10 established the staged PostgreSQL + ClickHouse + S3-compatible platform backbone—shared config, versioned migrations, bootstrap/doctor tooling, deterministic event/manifests, and a leased PostgreSQL outbox—without yet cutting the live product off SQLite.**

## What Happened

## What this slice actually delivered

S10 replaces the old “future platform” placeholder with a real, bootable polyglot foundation inside the repo.

T01 established a shared platform configuration boundary in `vinted_radar.platform.config`. PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and S3-compatible object-storage settings now load from one validated contract, storage prefixes are normalized safely, schema versions are explicit per provider, cutover flags are present but default to `false`, and redacted diagnostics expose endpoints/versions without leaking secrets.

T02 turned that contract into operator tooling. The repo now contains versioned PostgreSQL and ClickHouse migration directories, a local `docker-compose.data-platform.yml` stack for PostgreSQL + ClickHouse + MinIO, and `platform-bootstrap` / `platform-doctor` CLI commands that bootstrap schema state, create the object-store bucket when needed, ensure persistent `.prefix` marker objects, and run write/delete probes across the `raw_events`, `manifests`, and `parquet` prefixes.

T03 added the write-once contracts that downstream ingestion slices need. Listing-observed events now have deterministic UUIDv5 IDs, canonical JSON serialization, payload checksums, and stable object keys. Evidence manifests now carry deterministic IDs/checksums plus stable object keys. PostgreSQL now also owns a leased outbox contract keyed by unique `(event_id, sink)` rows so future sinks can claim, retry, and mark deliveries idempotently instead of inventing per-sink dedupe rules.

T04 closed the loop with a real smoke path. The Docker-backed proof boots PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and MinIO on isolated host ports, runs the real migrations, publishes a real outbox event, writes/reads a manifest object, and confirms that the same shared health renderer reports the platform healthy afterward.

## Patterns this slice established

- Keep the polyglot env contract centralized and staged: one config loader, explicit schema versions, redacted diagnostics, and cutover flags off by default until later slices are ready.
- Keep platform migration state provider-specific. PostgreSQL and ClickHouse can advance independently, so bootstrap/doctor must compare each provider to its own expected version.
- Treat object storage as a first-class platform dependency: bootstrap bucket state, persist prefix markers, and run write probes before future ingestion assumes the lake is writable.
- Treat event and manifest identities as deterministic contracts, not incidental row IDs, so evidence can be replayed, deduplicated, and joined across PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and object storage.
- Keep health rendering shared between the CLI and tests so operator diagnostics and proof text do not drift.

## What the next slices should know

S11-S13 should build on the contracts in this slice rather than inventing new ones. The safe path is: emit the deterministic event envelope once, attach deterministic evidence manifests, publish leased outbox rows per sink, and let PostgreSQL / ClickHouse / object storage consumers project from that write-once boundary. Do not bypass the outbox with sink-local heuristics, and do not collapse provider-specific schema versions back into one shared constant.

The product is intentionally still staged on SQLite. Nothing in S10 silently flips live reads or writes. Downstream slices must opt into PostgreSQL writes, ClickHouse writes, object-storage writes, and polyglot reads deliberately via the cutover flags after their projectors and serving paths are proven.

## Operational Readiness (Q8)

- **Health signal:** `python -m vinted_radar.cli platform-doctor` exits `0`, prints `Healthy: yes`, and reports PostgreSQL schema `v2`, ClickHouse schema `v1`, plus successful object-store write probes for `raw_events`, `manifests`, and `parquet`.
- **Failure signal:** `platform-doctor` exits non-zero and surfaces concrete reasons such as pending migrations, checksum drift, missing bucket/prefix markers, or failed object-store write probes; the smoke proof also fails if bootstrap/doctor output drifts from the shared renderer.

# S10: Polyglot Data Platform Foundation — UAT

**Milestone:** M002  
**Written:** 2026-03-28

## UAT Type

- UAT mode: local-platform-bootstrap
- Why this mode is sufficient: S10 is a backend/platform-foundation slice. The acceptance question is whether the repo can boot PostgreSQL + ClickHouse + MinIO locally, expose truthful bootstrap/doctor diagnostics, and prove deterministic event/outbox/manifests plumbing end to end.

## Preconditions

- Docker Desktop or a compatible Docker runtime is running locally.
- The repo dependencies are installed from `pyproject.toml`.
- No conflicting local services are bound to the default platform ports, or the operator has set the env-substituted overrides used by `infra/docker-compose.data-platform.yml` (`VINTED_RADAR_PLATFORM_POSTGRES_PORT`, `VINTED_RADAR_PLATFORM_CLICKHOUSE_HTTP_PORT`, `VINTED_RADAR_PLATFORM_CLICKHOUSE_NATIVE_PORT`, `VINTED_RADAR_PLATFORM_OBJECT_STORE_PORT`, `VINTED_RADAR_PLATFORM_OBJECT_STORE_CONSOLE_PORT`).
- Commands are run from the repo root.

## Test Cases

### 1. Boot the local data-platform stack

1. Run `docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.data-platform.yml up -d`.
2. Wait for the containers to become healthy.
3. **Expected:** PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and MinIO start successfully; if default ports are unavailable, the stack can still boot with the documented env-substituted port overrides.

### 2. Bootstrap schemas and object-storage prefixes

1. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli platform-bootstrap`.
2. Review the rendered report.
3. **Expected:** the command exits `0`, prints `Mode: bootstrap`, reports `PostgreSQL: ok`, `ClickHouse: ok`, `Object storage: ok`, and `Healthy: yes`, shows PostgreSQL migrations through `V002`, ClickHouse through `V001`, and confirms bucket/prefix readiness plus object-store write probes for `raw_events`, `manifests`, and `parquet`.

### 3. Verify steady-state platform health

1. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli platform-doctor`.
2. Review the rendered report.
3. **Expected:** the command exits `0`, prints `Mode: doctor`, keeps all three subsystems `ok`, preserves the same current schema versions (`postgres=2`, `clickhouse=1`), and reports successful write probes instead of pending migrations or bucket errors.

### 4. Verify the deterministic contract tests

1. Run `python -m pytest tests/test_event_envelope.py tests/test_outbox.py -q`.
2. **Expected:** all tests pass.
3. **Expected details:** the tests prove deterministic event IDs, deterministic manifest IDs/checksums, JSON round-trips, idempotent `(event_id, sink)` outbox publishing, leased claim/retry behavior, and delivery completion semantics.

### 5. Run the end-to-end platform smoke proof

1. Run `python -m pytest tests/test_data_platform_smoke.py -q`.
2. **Expected:** the test passes and proves a real end-to-end path: isolated Docker stack startup, `platform-bootstrap`, PostgreSQL V001/V002 application, ClickHouse V001 application, bucket/prefix readiness, a real outbox publish, a real manifest object write/read in MinIO, and a healthy `platform-doctor` result afterward.

## Edge Cases

### 6. Re-running bootstrap must remain idempotent

1. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli platform-bootstrap` a second time against the same running stack.
2. **Expected:** the command still exits `0` and stays healthy; already-applied migrations are not duplicated, and bucket/prefix checks remain clean.

### 7. Doctor must fail loudly on schema drift

1. Temporarily set an impossible expected version, for example `VINTED_RADAR_PLATFORM_POSTGRES_SCHEMA_VERSION=999` in the shell running the command.
2. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli platform-doctor`.
3. **Expected:** the command exits non-zero, prints `Healthy: no`, and surfaces PostgreSQL pending-version drift rather than silently reporting success.
4. Remove the override and rerun `python -m vinted_radar.cli platform-doctor`.
5. **Expected:** health returns to green.

## Failure Signals

- `platform-bootstrap` or `platform-doctor` exits non-zero.
- The rendered report shows `pending` migrations, checksum drift, `bucket-missing`, or failed write probes.
- The deterministic contract tests fail, indicating envelope/manifest IDs or outbox leasing semantics are no longer stable.
- The smoke test cannot publish the outbox event, cannot read/write the manifest object, or no longer reports the platform healthy after bootstrap.

## Requirements Proved By This UAT

- R016 — proves the project now has a production-style data-platform bootstrap, schema-versioning, and readiness-check foundation instead of relying only on the monolithic SQLite boundary.
- R014 (foundationally advanced, not validated) — proves the repo now has deterministic, evidence-linkable event and manifest contracts that future grounded AI/evidence slices can build on.

## Not Proven By This UAT

- Real evidence export into Parquet/object storage at production volume.
- PostgreSQL current-state projection or ClickHouse analytical serving.
- Historical SQLite backfill or full product cutover onto the new platform.
- Continuous remote/VPS monitoring or automated alerting.

## Cleanup

1. Run `docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.data-platform.yml down -v` when finished if the stack is no longer needed.
2. **Expected:** the local platform containers and volumes are removed cleanly.

## Notes for Tester

The quickest truthful acceptance loop for this slice is: boot the Docker stack, run `platform-bootstrap`, run `platform-doctor`, then run `python -m pytest tests/test_event_envelope.py tests/test_outbox.py tests/test_data_platform_smoke.py -q` if you want one compact proof of the deterministic contract plus the real stack path.

# S11: Minimal Evidence Contract + Parquet Lake

**S11 shrank raw collector proof to a minimal versioned evidence contract and moved it onto an immutable, inspectable Parquet/object-store lake with deterministic manifests, collector emission, and legacy SQLite backfill/lookup tooling.**

## What Happened

S11 turned the S10 platform foundation into the first real raw-evidence boundary for the project. T01 replaced the old API habit of copying full `dict(item)` payloads into SQLite with the same targeted proof posture already used for HTML cards: both parsers now emit a shared `schema_version` + `evidence_source` + `fragments` envelope, and `normalize_card_snapshot()` still understands legacy flat payloads so historical observation rows remain explainable while the forward-write contract gets dramatically smaller.

T02 added the immutable lake-writing layer on top of S10's event/manifest seams. Evidence batches are now anchored on deterministic batch `EventEnvelope`s, serialized to ZSTD-compressed Parquet, uploaded under deterministic object keys, and linked by uploaded manifests. The object-store helper now records SHA-256 metadata and refuses same-key/different-bytes rewrites, so the new Parquet lake behaves like an immutable evidence boundary rather than a mutable file bucket.

T03 connected real collector behavior to that boundary. Discovery now emits one deterministic listing-seen batch per accepted catalog page, state refresh emits one deterministic probe batch per refresh invocation, and both surfaces expose emitted batch metadata so operators and downstream slices can inspect what was published. The default factories auto-wire the shared publisher from platform cutover flags, and runtime/CLI cleanup now closes the full service rather than only the SQLite repository so the newly owned object-store/Postgres clients do not leak.

T04 closed the historical and debugging loop. Legacy SQLite discovery, observation, and probe rows can now be exported into the lake under explicit `vinted.backfill.*` event types, and `evidence-inspect` can resolve an event or manifest reference back down to the concrete Parquet row and decoded fragment path such as `row.raw_card.price.amount` or `row.detail.reason`. That means S12-S15 can treat the object-store lake as a real replay/debug surface now, not just a write-only archive.

The practical shape this slice established is: minimal evidence envelope -> deterministic batch event -> immutable Parquet object -> uploaded manifest -> inspectable event/manifest/row fragment drill-down. That is the seam downstream projector, warehouse, backfill, and grounded-AI slices should extend instead of inventing a second raw-evidence format.

## Verification

Re-ran every slice-plan verification command during closeout on 2026-03-29, and every command exited 0:

- `python -m pytest tests/test_api_catalog_page.py tests/test_card_payload.py -q` → **50 passed**. Re-proved the shared minimal evidence envelope plus backward-compatible normalization across new and legacy card payloads.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_lake_writer.py -q` → **2 passed, 1 skipped**. Re-proved deterministic manifested Parquet writing and immutable-key checksum protection; the Docker-gated MinIO integration case skipped in this workstation session because the local Docker daemon was unavailable.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_evidence_batches.py tests/test_discovery_service.py tests/test_runtime_service.py -q` → **20 passed**. Re-proved idempotent collector evidence publishing plus deterministic discovery/state-refresh batch emission without runtime regressions.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_evidence_export.py -q` → **3 passed**. Re-proved SQLite backfill, manifest/event lookup, and the operator-style `evidence-export` + `evidence-inspect` CLI round-trip.

This also satisfies the slice-level observability/diagnostic check: the closeout reran the dedicated export/inspect coverage that resolves lake objects back to concrete fragments, and the collector/report tests confirmed that discovery and state-refresh surface emitted batch metadata for later debugging.

## Requirements Advanced

- R014 — S11 moved raw proof onto a deterministic manifest + Parquet/object-store boundary and added fragment-level lookup, creating the durable evidence substrate future grounded AI summaries and inline insights can reference instead of SQLite-bound JSON blobs.
- R016 — S11 reduced hot-path storage pressure, introduced immutable lake retention with inspectable manifests, and added operator backfill/lookup tooling that advances the project toward a production-grade data platform.

## Requirements Validated

None.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

- T01 created `tests/test_card_payload.py` because the slice plan assumed a focused contract test file that was not present in the checkout.
- T02 reused the existing manifest model and CLI seams instead of adding extra domain or command wrappers that turned out not to be necessary.
- T04 streamed legacy rows directly from SQLite through the new export service instead of adding repository-only wrapper methods first.
- No slice replan was required.

## Known Limitations

- The live user-facing product still reads from the legacy SQLite boundary; S11 adds an immutable raw-evidence lake but does not yet cut mutable truth or serving reads over to PostgreSQL/ClickHouse.
- Object-store and outbox publishing remain staged behind platform configuration/cutover flags, so not every local collector invocation emits lake batches unless that platform path is enabled.
- Multi-row evidence batches require an explicit selector (`--row-index`, `--listing-id`, or `--probe-id`) for unambiguous inspection.
- This closeout session could not execute the Docker-gated MinIO case inside `tests/test_lake_writer.py` because the local Docker daemon was unavailable, although the command still passed and the deterministic/unit contract remained green.
- The slice adds strong pull-based inspection surfaces, but not continuous publish-lag metrics, alerting, or automated remediation for lake-write failures yet.

## Follow-ups

- S12 should consume the deterministic batch/manifests seam to project current-state and control-plane truth into PostgreSQL instead of extending SQLite mutation tables.
- S13 should build ClickHouse raw facts and serving rollups from the same manifested batch contract rather than introducing a second ingestion shape.
- S14 should use the explicit `vinted.backfill.*` export path to move the real historical SQLite corpus into the new platform and then cut live reads off the old SQLite history boundary.
- Re-run the Docker-backed MinIO lake verification path when a Docker daemon is available on the closeout workstation or target VPS environment.
- Add publish-failure visibility, lag metrics, and operator runbooks around the new evidence-lake path before the platform becomes the mandatory live boundary.

## Files Created/Modified

- `vinted_radar/card_payload.py` — Introduced the shared minimal listing-card evidence envelope builders and backward-compatible snapshot normalization.

1. Run `python -m pytest tests/test_lake_writer.py -q`.
2. **Expected:** deterministic batch event / parquet / manifest keys, ZSTD-compressed parquet, and immutable-key checksum protection.
3. **Expected details:** writing the same batch twice reuses identical objects, and reusing a key for different bytes is rejected. If Docker-backed MinIO is available, the MinIO round-trip case also passes instead of skipping.

### 3. Re-prove collector emission from discovery and state refresh

1. Run `python -m pytest tests/test_evidence_batches.py tests/test_discovery_service.py tests/test_runtime_service.py -q`.
2. **Expected:** the command passes.
3. **Expected details:** discovery emits one deterministic listing-seen batch per accepted catalog page, state refresh emits one deterministic probe batch per refresh invocation, emitted batch refs surface on service reports, and runtime regressions remain green.

### 4. Export legacy SQLite evidence into the Parquet lake

1. Seed a legacy smoke database:

   ```bash
   python - <<'PY'
   from pathlib import Path
   from tests.test_evidence_export import _seed_legacy_evidence_db
   path = Path('tmp/s11-uat-legacy.db')
   path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   _seed_legacy_evidence_db(path)
   print(path)
   PY
   ```

2. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli evidence-export --db tmp/s11-uat-legacy.db --format json`.
3. **Expected:** JSON output reports exactly three dataset groups (`discoveries`, `observations`, `probes`), explicit `vinted.backfill.*` event types, manifest/event IDs, row counts, and object keys under the configured lake prefixes.

### 5. Inspect exported evidence back down to a concrete fragment

1. From the previous JSON output, copy one discovery `event_id` and one probe `manifest_id`.
2. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli evidence-inspect --event-id <discovery-event-id> --listing-id 9001 --field-path row.raw_card.price.amount --format json`.
3. **Expected:** the output resolves the matching event, manifest, parquet object key, selected row, and fragment value `99.00`.
4. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli evidence-inspect --manifest-id <probe-manifest-id> --probe-id probe-20260328T200500-a1b2c3d4 --field-path row.detail.reason --format json`.
5. **Expected:** the output resolves the probe row and fragment value `anti_bot_challenge`.

## Edge Cases

### 6. Immutable-key protection must reject divergent rewrites

1. Re-run `python -m pytest tests/test_lake_writer.py -q` after modifying the batch payload in the relevant fixture or by calling the object-store helper with a different body under the same key.
2. **Expected:** the write is rejected with a checksum mismatch error rather than silently overwriting an existing object.

### 7. Evidence lookup must fail loudly on ambiguous multi-row batches

1. Run `python -m vinted_radar.cli evidence-inspect --event-id <discovery-event-id> --format json` without `--row-index`, `--listing-id`, or `--probe-id`.
2. **Expected:** the command exits non-zero and tells the operator to narrow the lookup because the batch contains multiple rows.

### 8. Direct CLI round-trip against the local MinIO stack

1. If Docker is available, start the local stack and bootstrap it:
   - `docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.data-platform.yml up -d`
   - `python -m vinted_radar.cli platform-bootstrap`
2. Repeat test cases 4 and 5 against the real local MinIO-backed object store.
3. **Expected:** export and inspect behave the same as the fixture-backed path, proving the operator-facing lake commands work end to end on the real stack.

## Failure Signals

- Parser/normalization tests start persisting heavyweight raw-card payloads again or stop accepting legacy payloads.
- Lake writer tests show non-deterministic object keys, missing manifests, or silent same-key overwrite behavior.
- Discovery/state-refresh tests stop surfacing emitted batch refs or lose idempotence across retries.
- `evidence-export` stops producing explicit `vinted.backfill.*` event types or `evidence-inspect` can no longer resolve `row.raw_card.*` / `row.detail.*` fragments.

## Requirements Proved By This UAT

- R016 — advances the production-style storage/operability posture by moving immutable raw proof onto a Parquet/object-store boundary with deterministic manifests and inspectable export tooling.
- R014 (advanced, not validated) — gives future grounded AI and evidence-linked summary work a stable raw-evidence contract and replayable lookup path instead of SQLite-bound JSON blobs.

## Not Proven By This UAT

- PostgreSQL current-state projection and control-plane cutover.
- ClickHouse serving warehouse and rollups.
- Full historical backfill of the real large SQLite corpus.
- Automated monitoring/alerting around lake-publish lag or failures.

## Cleanup

1. Remove `tmp/s11-uat-legacy.db` if it is no longer needed.
2. If Docker-backed MinIO was used, stop the stack with `docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.data-platform.yml down -v`.

slices.integration_closure: Runtime commands, discovery bookkeeping, catalogs, listing current state, presence summaries, manifests, and outbox checkpoints read/write through PostgreSQL-backed repositories and projectors, while the old SQLite path remains only a migration input.

slices.title: Historical Backfill + Application Cutover

# T03: Added deterministic listing event envelopes, evidence manifests, and a leased PostgreSQL outbox contract.

**Added deterministic listing event envelopes, evidence manifests, and a leased PostgreSQL outbox contract.**

## What Happened

Built the durable event/outbox foundation for the polyglot platform slice. Added a versioned `EventEnvelope` with canonical JSON serialization, deterministic UUIDv5 event IDs, payload checksums, storage-key helpers, and a concrete `build_listing_observed_event(...)` constructor over the current `ListingCard` discovery shape. Added deterministic evidence-manifest contracts with per-object checksums and storage keys, then introduced a PostgreSQL V002 migration plus `PostgresOutbox` publish/claim/fail/deliver flows so future sinks can consume unique `(event_id, sink)` rows with lease-based retries instead of ad hoc dedupe. Because PostgreSQL advanced to V002 while ClickHouse stayed on V001, I also split provider-specific schema-version defaults, updated bootstrap/config regressions, exported the outbox seam, documented the new baseline, recorded D036, and appended the schema-version-default gotcha to `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md`.

## Verification

`python -m pytest tests/test_event_envelope.py tests/test_outbox.py -q` passed, covering deterministic envelope IDs, JSON round-trips, manifest ID/checksum stability, idempotent outbox publish semantics, leased claim/retry behavior, and delivery completion. `python -m pytest tests/test_platform_config.py tests/test_data_platform_bootstrap.py -q` also passed because this task introduced a real PostgreSQL V002 migration and provider-specific schema-version defaults. A final combined run of all four platform tests passed as well.

# T04: Added a Docker-backed platform smoke proof with shared health rendering and isolated compose fixtures.

**Added a Docker-backed platform smoke proof with shared health rendering and isolated compose fixtures.**

## What Happened

Implemented the final proof layer for the S10 platform foundation. I parameterized `infra/docker-compose.data-platform.yml` so PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and MinIO can bind to unique host ports during tests, which also removed the real ClickHouse/MinIO host-port collision on `9000`. I added `vinted_radar.platform.health` plus package exports and switched the CLI to render platform bootstrap/doctor diagnostics through those shared helpers, so both operator output and test assertions read the same readiness surface. I expanded `tests/conftest.py` with Docker-aware smoke fixtures, ephemeral compose project setup, backend client factories, and captured subprocess diagnostics, then added `tests/test_data_platform_smoke.py` to boot the real stack, run `platform-bootstrap`, verify PostgreSQL and ClickHouse migrations, publish a real outbox event with a manifest row, write a manifest JSON object to MinIO, and confirm `platform-doctor` plus direct health summaries stay green afterward. During live execution the Windows environment also exposed that bare `psycopg` could not load `libpq`, so I pinned `psycopg[binary]` in `pyproject.toml` and recorded that workstation-specific setup rule in `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md`.

## Verification

`python -m pytest tests/test_data_platform_smoke.py -q` passed against the real local Docker stack, proving isolated-port compose startup, PostgreSQL V001/V002 and ClickHouse V001 migration application, MinIO bucket/prefix bootstrap, a real outbox publish into PostgreSQL, a manifest JSON write/read in object storage, and healthy `platform-doctor` / shared health rendering afterward. I then ran `python -m pytest tests/test_platform_config.py tests/test_data_platform_bootstrap.py tests/test_event_envelope.py tests/test_outbox.py tests/test_data_platform_smoke.py -q`, and all 15 platform-foundation tests passed as the final S10 regression set.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `python -m pytest tests/test_data_platform_smoke.py -q` | 0 | ✅ pass | 49720ms |

## Deviations

Expanded the task slightly beyond the original file list to parameterize compose host ports and pin `psycopg[binary]` in `pyproject.toml`, because the live smoke run surfaced a real host-port collision and a Windows `libpq` loader failure that would have made the planned proof non-executable. No slice replan was needed.

## Known Issues

The smoke/regression pytest runs still emit one transitive Python 3.13 deprecation warning (`'u'` type code) from imported dependency code, but all platform verification passes.

# T01: Replaced full API raw-card persistence with a shared minimal evidence envelope and normalization tests.

**Replaced full API raw-card persistence with a shared minimal evidence envelope and normalization tests.**

## What Happened

Introduced a shared minimal listing-card evidence contract in vinted_radar/card_payload.py using a versioned envelope with schema_version, evidence_source, and fragments. Switched the HTML parser to emit the shared envelope and replaced the API parser's old raw_card=dict(item) behavior with a targeted fragment builder that preserves only title/brand/size/status/price proof while dropping heavy nested payloads from the hot path. Expanded normalize_card_snapshot() so repository hydration accepts both the new envelope and historical flat HTML/API payloads, then added focused tests for envelope generation, snapshot normalization, and legacy compatibility. # T02: Added an immutable S3-backed Parquet batch writer with uploaded manifests and MinIO round-trip tests.

**Added an immutable S3-backed Parquet batch writer with uploaded manifests and MinIO round-trip tests.**

## What Happened

Added vinted_radar/platform/object_store.py with a reusable checksum-aware S3-compatible object-store client that can create buckets, upload/download bytes/text/JSON, expose manifest-ready object metadata, and reject same-key rewrites when the recorded SHA-256 differs. Added vinted_radar/platform/lake_writer.py with a ParquetLakeWriter that anchors each evidence batch on a batch EventEnvelope, stages ZSTD-compressed Parquet locally, uploads the batch event JSON plus the partitioned Parquet file, and publishes an EvidenceManifest that references both objects under deterministic keys. Exported the new platform types through vinted_radar/platform/__init__.py, added unit and MinIO-backed integration coverage in tests/test_lake_writer.py, and recorded the immutable-key checksum rule in project knowledge plus architecture decision D039.

## Verification

Ran the task-plan verification command `python -m pytest tests/test_lake_writer.py -q`. It passed with three green checks covering deterministic manifest/parquet writing against a fake S3 backend, immutable key rejection for divergent rewrites, and a real MinIO round-trip that restored the batch event, manifest, and Parquet rows from object storage.

tasks.title: Added PostgreSQL V003 mutable-truth schema for runtime, discovery, catalogs, listing state, manifests, and projector checkpoints.
tasks.one_liner: Added PostgreSQL V003 mutable-truth schema for runtime, discovery, catalogs, listing state, manifests, and projector checkpoints.
tasks.narrative: Implemented the PostgreSQL mutable-truth schema required for S12 by adding `infra/postgres/migrations/V003__platform_mutable_truth.sql` and a reusable `vinted_radar.platform.postgres_schema` contract module. # T01: Added PostgreSQL V003 mutable-truth schema for runtime, discovery, catalogs, listing state, manifests, and projector checkpoints.

**Added PostgreSQL V003 mutable-truth schema for runtime, discovery, catalogs, listing state, manifests, and projector checkpoints.**

## What Happened

Implemented the PostgreSQL mutable-truth schema required for S12 by adding `infra/postgres/migrations/V003__platform_mutable_truth.sql` and a reusable `vinted_radar.platform.postgres_schema` contract module. The migration adds projector-safe tables for mutable manifests, discovery runs, runtime cycles, runtime controller truth, catalogs, listing identity, listing current state, listing presence summaries, and composite-key outbox checkpoints, all keyed so later projectors can upsert idempotently on natural IDs instead of inventing surrogate dedupe layers. I also moved the repository’s default PostgreSQL baseline to schema v3, re-exported the schema contract through `vinted_radar.platform`, and updated the existing platform config/bootstrap/smoke regressions so normal bootstrap flows now expect the new migration instead of leaving it disconnected from the live platform path.

## Verification

`python -m pytest tests/test_postgres_schema.py -q` passed, proving the real migration runner applies V003 and that the expected mutable-truth tables, indexes, natural keys, and composite checkpoint key exist. `python -m pytest tests/test_platform_config.py tests/test_data_platform_bootstrap.py -q` also passed, proving the version bump is wired into the repository’s default PostgreSQL baseline and bootstrap fixtures.

## Deviations

Updated the platform default schema version and existing platform bootstrap/smoke regression tests in addition to the new task-specific test file, because leaving the default PostgreSQL baseline at V002 would have made the V003 migration unreachable in the normal bootstrap path.

decisions.decision: Market coverage focus
decisions.choice: Vinted Homme and Femme categories plus all reachable sub-categories only
decisions.rationale: Keeps the first radar focused enough to cover deeply and compare contextually.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if a later milestone explicitly expands marketplace scope

decisions.decision: Data acquisition backbone
decisions.choice: Public collection only, no login required, and no central dependency on undocumented private APIs
decisions.rationale: Matches access constraints and reduces fragility risk.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if an official supported data channel becomes available and complements public collection

decisions.decision: Truth surface in product
decisions.choice: Separate observed facts, inferred conclusions, and uncertain states everywhere that matters
decisions.rationale: Product credibility depends on making evidence and uncertainty explicit.

decisions.decision: Primary product view
decisions.choice: Mixed dashboard with market summary first and listing-level proof immediately underneath
decisions.rationale: The product must tell a market story and let the user verify it quickly.

decisions.decision: Local runtime modes
decisions.choice: Support both batch mode and continuous mode in M001
decisions.rationale: Batch supports testing and controlled reruns; continuous mode is required for a living radar.

decisions.decision: Listing state model posture
decisions.choice: Use a prudent state machine covering active, sold observed/probable, unavailable non-conclusive, deleted when distinct, and unknown
decisions.rationale: Avoids overclaiming from ambiguous public signals.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if live evidence later justifies tighter or broader state distinctions

decisions.decision: Ranking split
decisions.choice: Provide separate “demande pure” and “premium” rankings
decisions.rationale: These answer different market questions and should not collapse into one score.

decisions.decision: Premium score weighting
decisions.choice: Price acts as an intelligent contextual factor, not the dominant driver
decisions.rationale: The premium ranking should reward strong performance at relatively high price, not simply expensive listings.

decisions.decision: Milestone sequencing
decisions.choice: Build listing-level credibility first, then richer market reading, then product-level intelligence plus AI, then SaaS hardening
decisions.rationale: Preserves the evidence backbone before richer analytics and commercialization.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if execution proves a different sequence is materially safer or faster

decisions.decision: S01 collector delivery stack
decisions.choice: Implement S01 as a Python package with a Typer batch CLI, public vinted.com HTML extraction over HTTP, SQLite persistence, and targeted raw evidence fragments rather than full-page archives by default.
decisions.rationale: The repository is a blank slate, public SSR HTML already exposes enough catalog and item data for S01, SQLite keeps the system local-first, and targeted evidence retention preserves explainability without creating unnecessary archive volume.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if public HTML extraction stops being viable or later slices need broader evidence retention.

decisions.decision: Public acquisition shape for S01
decisions.choice: Use Vinted's server-rendered `/catalog` HTML plus the embedded `catalogTree` flight payload for seed sync, and ingest listing cards from SSR `new-item-box__container` fragments into SQLite with targeted raw evidence retention.
decisions.rationale: Live verification showed the public catalog page already exposes the full Homme/Femme category tree and enough listing-card fields to bootstrap discovery without browser automation or undocumented private APIs. This keeps the collector local-first, lowers anti-bot surface area, and preserves inspectable evidence without full-page archives.

decisions.decision: Observation history granularity for S02
decisions.choice: Persist `listing_observations` as one normalized observation per listing per run, while keeping `listing_discoveries` as the per-sighting diagnostic layer.
decisions.rationale: Per-run observations make first seen/last seen/cadence/freshness truthful and cheap to query, while the detailed discovery rows still preserve the lower-level evidence needed for debugging category/page behavior.

decisions.decision: State evidence precedence for S03
decisions.choice: Treat direct public item-page probe evidence as highest-priority state input: 404/410 map to `deleted`, parsed buy-state flags map to observed `active`/`sold`/`unavailable`, and only unresolved cases fall back to cautious history-based inference from follow-up misses.
decisions.rationale: This keeps state claims grounded in the strongest public signal available while preserving prudent fallback behavior when public item pages are inconclusive or unchanged. It also keeps browserless collection viable by reusing escaped script data already present in the public HTML.

decisions.decision: S04 ranking model posture
decisions.choice: Keep `demand` and `premium` as separate on-demand score surfaces; demand is anchored in state/history evidence, and premium reuses demand as the base score with only a modest contextual price boost when the peer group clears explicit support thresholds.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the distinction between sell-through and high-price performance, keeps price from dominating the premium ranking, and avoids fake contextual precision when peer support is thin.

decisions.decision: S05 dashboard delivery architecture
decisions.choice: Serve the local dashboard as a server-rendered standard-library WSGI app with matching JSON diagnostics, reusing repository/state/scoring payloads instead of duplicating business logic in client code.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the product local-first and dependency-light, and makes the UI more truthful because the browser surface and the debug endpoints are driven by the exact same server-side payload assembly.

decisions.decision: S06 local operator runtime observability architecture
decisions.choice: Persist each batch or continuous radar cycle in SQLite `runtime_cycles` and expose that same runtime truth through `runtime-status`, `/api/runtime`, and the dashboard runtime card.
decisions.rationale: Continuous mode needs durable phase/error visibility without diverging into separate scheduler state files; keeping runtime-cycle truth in SQLite lets the CLI and dashboard read the same source of truth and preserves failure context when a cycle dies mid-run.

decisions.decision: Primary acquisition backbone after the March 2026 review
decisions.choice: Use Vinted's private `/api/v2/catalog/items` endpoint as the primary discovery surface, while keeping public item pages as the separate state-evidence path.
decisions.rationale: The user explicitly chose to assume the private API tradeoff. It materially improves catalog discovery throughput and field richness, and the project already retains public item-page probes as an independent evidence layer for cautious state resolution.

decisions.decision: How synchronized SQLite databases are promoted into the local working path
decisions.choice: Create a consistent remote snapshot first, copy it to a temporary local file, run database health checks, then atomically rename it into place.
decisions.rationale: The project runs SQLite in WAL mode and handles very large live databases. Directly copying into `data/vinted-radar.db` can expose partial, WAL-incomplete, or corrupted transfers as if they were usable local truth.

decisions.decision: M002 delivery stack
decisions.choice: Keep the existing Python + SQLite + server-rendered WSGI architecture for M002 and deepen repository-driven SQL payloads instead of doing a framework rewrite.
decisions.rationale: The codebase already has the right brownfield seams in RadarRepository, the WSGI route split, and SQLite runtime persistence. The milestone risk is product shape, payload scalability, runtime truth, and VPS serving discipline—not a missing frontend framework.

decisions.decision: How the M002/S01 overview home retires the current home-path scale risk
decisions.choice: Implement S01 around a repository-owned SQL overview contract for `/`, while keeping the existing WSGI route topology and `/api/dashboard` seam as the brownfield compatibility surface.
decisions.rationale: The current default home still loads listing-state inputs across the full corpus and assembles market summary modules in Python. S01 needs to retire that primary-path risk without a framework rewrite or a second persisted state cache. Reusing the existing `/`, `/explorer`, and `/api/...` route split while moving overview summaries and first comparison modules into repository SQL keeps the change incremental, testable, and compatible with later S02-S05 slices.

decisions.decision: How the M002/S01 overview payload derives state counts and first comparison modules without full-corpus scoring.
decisions.choice: Implement the overview home contract as a repository-owned `overview_snapshot()` built from SQL state classification that reuses `state_machine` thresholds/constants, and keep thin-support comparison rows visible with explicit low-support flags instead of filtering them away.
decisions.rationale: This retires the current home-path scale risk while keeping the evidence vocabulary aligned with the existing Python state machine. Surfacing low-support rows and module reasons preserves honesty and makes empty/thin modules diagnosable in JSON instead of disappearing silently.

decisions.decision: Brownfield compatibility posture for the M002/S01 overview home
decisions.choice: Keep `/api/dashboard` as the machine-readable compatibility endpoint while making `/` a SQL-backed French overview, and source featured listing drill-downs from the SQL explorer page instead of the legacy scored home payload.
decisions.rationale: This preserves existing dashboard diagnostics and caller integrations while retiring full-corpus Python recomputation from the primary home path. It also keeps overview drill-down links SQL-first end to end, so future polish on `/` does not quietly reintroduce the old scoring-heavy assembly path.

decisions.decision: How S02 exposes pause/resume control versus runtime visibility
decisions.choice: Make pause/resume a CLI-driven, DB-persisted control surface in S02 and keep `/runtime` plus `/api/runtime` read-only visibility surfaces.
decisions.rationale: The dashboard is currently GET-only and the slice's primary risk is truthful persistence and orchestration, not HTTP mutation handling. CLI controls are lower churn, easier to test, and still let the runtime page, overview, and API read the same persisted truth.

decisions.decision: How S02 exposes pause/resume control versus runtime visibility
decisions.choice: Make pause/resume a CLI-driven, DB-persisted control surface in S02 and keep `/runtime` plus `/api/runtime` read-only visibility surfaces.
decisions.rationale: The dashboard is currently GET-only and the slice's primary risk is truthful persistence and orchestration, not HTTP mutation handling. CLI controls are lower churn, easier to test, and still let the runtime page, overview, and API read the same persisted truth.

decisions.decision: How M002/S02 represents current runtime truth
decisions.choice: Add a separate persisted `runtime_controller_state` snapshot/table for current controller and scheduler truth, while keeping `runtime_cycles` as immutable per-cycle history.
decisions.rationale: Scheduled, paused, next-resume, elapsed-pause, and heartbeat truth cannot be represented honestly by overloading the last cycle row. A separate controller snapshot fits the brownfield SQLite schema/migration model, lets the dashboard and scraper agree across separate processes, and preserves cycle history semantics.

decisions.decision: How continuous mode orders post-cycle controller transitions versus observer callbacks
decisions.choice: Persist the next controller state (`scheduled` / `paused`) before observer-facing callbacks after each continuous cycle.
decisions.rationale: Live S02 smoke proved that calling observer callbacks before writing the next controller state can create a transient truth gap between `/api/runtime`, `/health`, and the overview/runtime UI. Persisting the transition first keeps all read surfaces aligned on the same SQLite truth.

decisions.decision: How S03 represents mounted VPS serving and route verification
decisions.choice: Use a shared `base_path` + optional `public_base_url` serving contract across the CLI, SSR route generation, health payloads, systemd installer, and the `verify_vps_serving.py` smoke verifier.
decisions.rationale: The live product now needs to work behind a reverse-proxy prefix without localhost-era links or route drift. One shared serving contract keeps HTML, JSON diagnostics, CLI output, and operator docs aligned, while the smoke verifier gives future agents one repeatable proof command for the mounted path.

decisions.decision: How M002/S04 makes `/explorer` the main browse-and-compare workspace without reintroducing request-time full-corpus reshaping.
decisions.choice: Build S04 around one repository-owned classified explorer snapshot that feeds filters, paging, comparison modules, and detail-navigation context, while keeping thin-support rows visible with explicit honesty metadata instead of suppressing them.
decisions.rationale: A single SQL-first explorer contract keeps filtering, paging, support counts, and drill-down links truthful across `/explorer`, `/api/explorer`, overview modules, and listing detail. Preserving the active explorer query in detail links closes the analytical loop, and leaving low-support rows visible avoids fake precision or silent disappearance of weak slices.

decisions.decision: How M002/S06 represents degraded acquisition truth across product and runtime surfaces
decisions.choice: Persist state-refresh degradation summaries on each runtime cycle and aggregate them with latest discovery scan failures in `RadarRepository.runtime_status().acquisition`; keep JSON diagnostics literal and translate only the visible product wording in `dashboard.py`.
decisions.rationale: S06 needs one durable honesty contract for anti-bot hits, degraded probes, and failed scans. Keeping that truth in repository/runtime payloads prevents overview, explorer, detail, runtime, `/api/runtime`, and `/health` from drifting into page-local heuristics, while the product layer can still translate the same signals into broader-audience French copy.

decisions.when_context: M002/S05 architecture restored during M002/S06 closeout
decisions.decision: Restore the S05 detail-route architecture decision in the append-only register after the S06 decision was appended
decisions.choice: Keep `/api/listings/<id>` as the raw proof contract but add a narrative/provenance layer in `dashboard.py`, and render the HTML detail route as narrative-first with progressive proof disclosures.
decisions.rationale: The S05 architectural choice is still active project knowledge and should remain explicitly recorded alongside the new S06 degraded-acquisition decision. Keeping both decisions visible prevents future agents from inferring that S06 replaced the narrative/progressive-proof detail architecture.

decisions.decision: How discovery should apply price filters to Vinted catalog scans
decisions.choice: Thread configured min/max price bounds into Vinted's catalog API request and retain a local post-download price guard before persistence.
decisions.rationale: Applying price bounds at the API boundary reduces wasted HTTP traffic and ban pressure, while the local guard preserves correctness if Vinted still returns a few out-of-range cards.

decisions.decision: How M002/S09 should turn a large Webshare proxy pool into real acquisition throughput without leaking credentials
decisions.choice: Adopt a gitignored local proxy-file contract (`data/proxies.txt`), normalize raw `host:port:user:pass` entries centrally, drive discovery through route-local sync/async sessions with per-route warm-up/throttling/cooldowns, and expose only safe pool metadata plus a `proxy-preflight` operator check
decisions.rationale: The existing retry-only proxy rotation barely used a large pool and forced operators to rewrite Webshare exports by hand. Route-local transport state lets concurrent discovery actually spread across the pool, while the local file contract keeps credentials out of the repo and the runtime/CLI surfaces stay honest by persisting only pool size and transport mode. A preflight command closes the operator loop by proving exit-route diversity and Vinted reachability before longer runs.

decisions.when_context: M002 roadmap extension after S09 / VPS storage-growth incident
decisions.decision: Long-term storage and analytics architecture after the SQLite growth failure
decisions.choice: Adopt a polyglot data platform: PostgreSQL for mutable control-plane and current-state truth, ClickHouse for analytical/serving facts and rollups, and S3-compatible object storage with Parquet for immutable raw evidence; keep SQLite only as a migration/backfill source, not the live durable boundary.
decisions.rationale: The live VPS incident showed that one successful continuous cycle can grow the cleaned SQLite database by roughly 1.5 GB while the current schema duplicates heavyweight card payload JSON across listings, listing_discoveries, and listing_observations. SQLite is no longer a credible long-term boundary for mutable runtime state, append-heavy analytical history, and raw evidence retention at the same time. Splitting those concerns gives bounded OLTP state in PostgreSQL, low-latency analytical serving and rollups in ClickHouse, and cheap durable cold retention in Parquet on object storage. This also creates AI-ready feature surfaces without forcing future models to scan raw event history directly.
decisions.revisable: Yes — vendor and table-format details can evolve, but the separation between mutable state, serving analytics, and immutable raw evidence should remain.

decisions.decision: How the polyglot platform connection/config contract is represented in code
decisions.choice: Use one dataclass-based `vinted_radar.platform.config` loader for PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and S3-compatible settings, including normalized object prefixes, explicit schema versions, redacted observability output, and cutover flags that all default to false.
decisions.rationale: Future bootstrap, migration, doctor, and outbox slices need one validated source of truth for platform endpoints and versions, but the current product must not silently switch live reads or writes. A single loader keeps the contract consistent and testable while preserving a safe staged-cutover posture.

decisions.decision: How bootstrap and doctor track schema/object-store readiness for the polyglot platform foundation
decisions.choice: Use versioned `V###__name.sql` directories with per-provider `platform_schema_migrations` ledgers, plus persistent `.prefix` marker objects and write/delete probes for the configured S3-compatible prefixes.
decisions.rationale: This keeps bootstrap idempotent across PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and object storage, makes expected schema versions observable from config, and exposes drift or missing write access before later outbox/manifest slices depend on the platform.

decisions.decision: How the polyglot platform identifies events/manifests and guarantees idempotent multi-sink delivery
decisions.choice: Use canonical-JSON versioned event envelopes with deterministic UUIDv5 IDs, deterministic SHA-256 manifest checksums/IDs, and a PostgreSQL outbox keyed by unique `(event_id, sink)` rows with leased claim/retry state.
decisions.rationale: Future ingestion and projection slices need one write-once contract that can be replayed safely into multiple sinks without per-sink dedupe heuristics. Canonical payload hashing keeps the envelope and manifest bodies stable, while the outbox lease/retry state makes delivery observable and idempotent across retries and parallel workers.

decisions.decision: How S10 proves and renders platform health end to end
decisions.choice: Use env-substituted host ports, unique Docker compose project names, and shared health-rendering helpers so the real smoke stack stays isolated and platform-bootstrap/platform-doctor expose the exact readiness text the smoke proof asserts.
decisions.rationale: The live Docker-backed proof surfaced real workstation-specific collisions and setup drift risks. Isolating host ports and compose project names keeps the smoke path repeatable on shared machines, while shared health rendering prevents the CLI and tests from drifting into different platform-readiness vocabularies.

decisions.decision: Listing-card raw evidence contract for catalog ingestion
decisions.choice: Persist versioned minimal evidence envelopes (`schema_version`, `evidence_source`, `fragments`) for both HTML and API cards, and keep normalization backward-compatible with legacy flat payloads.
decisions.rationale: This removes heavyweight API payload copies from the hot mutable path while preserving the targeted fragments needed to explain normalized listing fields. Backward-compatible normalization prevents historical observation rows and older fixtures from becoming unreadable when the evidence contract evolves.

decisions.decision: How evidence-lake batches are published and referenced across object storage and manifests
decisions.choice: Anchor each evidence batch on a deterministic batch EventEnvelope, upload that JSON plus the partitioned Parquet object under deterministic keys, and reject same-key rewrites when the recorded SHA-256 differs.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the Parquet lake immutable and idempotent, lets EvidenceManifest stay compatible with the existing event_id/outbox seam, and gives downstream slices one stable object graph to inspect or replay: batch event JSON -> Parquet batch -> manifest.

decisions.decision: How collector services enable evidence-lake emission and surface emitted batches
decisions.choice: Discovery and state-refresh now auto-create a shared collector evidence publisher from platform cutover flags in the default factories, emit deterministic Parquet/manifests batches when object-storage writes are enabled, publish outbox rows when Postgres writes are enabled, and expose emitted batch metadata on service reports.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the default runtime path connected to the new evidence lake without forcing platform dependencies in tests or local direct-mode runs, preserves idempotence through deterministic event identities plus immutable object keys/outbox conflicts, and gives downstream operators and slices a concrete report surface for manifest/event inspection.

decisions.decision: Historical evidence export event typing for the Parquet lake
decisions.choice: Emit legacy SQLite discovery, observation, and probe exports under explicit `vinted.backfill.*` batch event types with `capture_source=sqlite_backfill` metadata instead of reusing live collector event names
decisions.rationale: This keeps historical lake exports distinguishable from live discovery/state-refresh evidence during replay, debugging, and future S14 cutover/backfill work, while still reusing the deterministic event and manifest contracts introduced in S10.

decisions.decision: Evidence lookup inspection posture for manifested Parquet batches
decisions.choice: Decode canonical-JSON Parquet cell strings back into navigable nested `row.*`, `event.*`, and `manifest.*` fragments during evidence inspection
decisions.rationale: The immutable lake stores many evidence/details cells as canonical JSON strings inside Parquet rows. Decoding them on lookup keeps the lake inspectable down to concrete fragments like `row.raw_card.price.amount` or `row.detail.reason` without bespoke per-dataset parsers.

decisions.decision: How PostgreSQL mutable truth is decomposed for control-plane and current-state projection
decisions.choice: Use separate PostgreSQL tables for mutable manifests, discovery runs, runtime cycles/controller state, catalogs, listing identity, listing current state, listing presence summaries, and composite-key outbox checkpoints, with natural primary keys and projector-facing provenance fields (`last_event_id`, `last_manifest_id`).
decisions.rationale: The SQLite codebase already distinguishes stable listing identity, current state, recent presence, runtime controller truth, and cycle history. Preserving that separation in PostgreSQL keeps projector upserts idempotent on natural keys, avoids re-duplicating raw evidence blobs in hot mutable tables, and gives later runtime/CLI cutover work explicit checkpoint and provenance surfaces for replay, lag inspection, and troubleshooting.
